name: mcp-auth
description: Multi-method authentication for MCP servers: Device Auth Flow, Client Credentials, Password Grant, Basic Auth, and API Key — per-server config, per-IDP settings, injected into VS Code MCP config automatically.

method.description:
Authentication method for this server. If omitted, you will be prompted on first sign-in.
• device — OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Flow (browser login)
• client_credentials — grant_type=client_credentials (client_id + client_secret)
• password — grant_type=password (client_id + client_secret + username + password)
• basic — HTTP Basic Authorization header
• api_key — static key in a configurable header

clientId.description: OAuth client_id — used by device and client_credentials methods. Not sensitive.

deviceAuthEndpoint.description: Device Authorization Endpoint URL — used by device method only.

tokenEndpoint.description: Token Endpoint URL — used by device and client_credentials methods.

scopes.description: Space-separated OAuth scopes. Defaults to 'openid profile email' for device flow.

username.description: Username for Basic Auth. Not sensitive; password is always prompted and stored in SecretStorage.

apiKeyHeader.description: Header name to inject the API key into (e.g. 'x-api-key', 'Authorization'). Defaults to 'x-api-key'.

allowInsecureTls.description: Skip TLS certificate verification for this server's auth endpoints. Use only behind a corporate proxy with a self-signed certificate.

mcpAuth.tokenRefreshBuffer.description: Seconds before token expiry to proactively refresh. Applies to device and client_credentials tokens.

mcpAuth.allowInsecureTls.description: Global TLS verification bypass. Per-server allowInsecureTls in serverConfigs takes precedence.
